search-options.heading: Search Options
search-options.open-new-tab: Open result links in new tab
search-options.open-new-tab-aria: Open result links in new tab
search-options.inline-gif-playback: Disable GIF animation in image results
search-options.inline-gif-playback-aria: Disable GIF animation in image results
search-options.post-method: Use POST method for search requests (Won't work with streaming results)
search-options.post-method-aria: Use POST method for search requests (Won't work with streaming results)
search-options.post-method-tooltip: POST method is not compatible with streaming results

install.heading: Install app
install.desc: Add degoog to your home screen. If your browser did not show the install prompt, use this to try again. This won't work unless you are serving your site via https and your browser supports PWA.

update-check.heading: Version
update-check.desc: is currently installed. The newest version available is

presets.heading: Server presets
presets.desc: Apply a one-shot template for common deployment styles. Presets override the listed settings, but they do not set environment variables or replace your custom lists.
presets.no-changes: This preset already matches the managed settings.

personal-local.label: Personal / Local Only
personal-local.desc: For single-user LAN or homelab deployments where convenience matters and the network is trusted.

private-team.label: Family / Semi-Lax
private-team.desc: For a small trusted set of people behind auth, VPN, or a private reverse proxy.

public-web.label: Public Web Instance
public-web.desc: For a normal public-facing instance with streaming, honeypot checks, and sensible public rate limits.

hardened-public.label: Hardened Public Instance
hardened-public.desc: For exposed instances expecting abuse, with stricter limits and API key enforcement toggles enabled.

indexer-discovery.label: Indexer / Discovery Mode
indexer-discovery.desc: For instances prioritizing discovery features and local result recording while keeping basic public safeguards.

compat-low-resource.label: Compatibility / Low Resource Mode
compat-low-resource.desc: For older browsers, buffering proxies, small VPSes, or simpler request behavior with streaming disabled.

warnings.local-only: Use this only on trusted local networks. Do not expose it to the public web as-is.
warnings.private-team: Put this behind auth, VPN, or a reverse proxy and avoid casually exposing admin/settings routes.
warnings.public-password: Set DEGOOG_SETTINGS_PASSWORDS in the environment. The UI cannot set it for you.
warnings.public-https: Use HTTPS and a properly configured reverse proxy for exposed deployments.
warnings.public-monitor: Monitor abuse and avoid exposing admin/settings casually.
warnings.api-key: API key enforcement only helps when clients are configured to send valid signed requests and the key is protected.
warnings.hardened: Consider proxy, WAF, container, and host hardening for hostile traffic.
warnings.indexer-storage: Indexer mode needs storage/backend planning before you rely on it.
warnings.compat: Non-streaming can feel slower and less lively, but it is easier to debug and works better with some proxies.

server.cache-desc: Flush stored cache entries. Search and autocomplete are kept separately; extensions register their own caches (e.g. wiki, ai-summary).
server.api-key-no-password: Set a password via the DEGOOG_SETTINGS_PASSWORDS environment variable to view and manage the API key.
server.api-key-desc: Protect search and suggestion endpoints with a signed nonce derived from this key. Enable the toggles below to require a valid nonce on those routes.
server.api-key-search-tooltip: Require a valid signed nonce on /api/search, /api/search/stream and /api/search/retry
server.api-key-suggest-tooltip: Require a valid signed nonce on /api/suggest and /api/suggest/opensearch
server.streaming-desc: Load results progressively as each engine responds instead of waiting for all engines to finish. This is an experimental feature.
server.streaming-enable-tooltip: Streaming is not compatible with POST search method
server.languages-desc: Change the search languages in the search options dropdown. When disabled, all ~90 built-in languages are shown.
server.proxy-desc: Use proxies for server-side search requests. One URL per line; requests rotate through the list.
server.image-proxy-allow-local-aria: Allow the image proxy to reach local network addresses
server.image-proxy-allow-list-label: Allowed local hosts (one regex per line)
server.image-proxy-allow-list-desc: By default the image proxy blocks private and local addresses. Add regex patterns matching the hosts or IPs you trust (e.g. self-hosted Jellyfin or Meilisearch). Leave empty to allow every local address at your own risk.
server.rate-limit-desc: Limit requests per IP to protect the instance and upstream engines.
server.rate-limit-defaults: Defaults: 15 requests per 20s, 150 per 10 min per IP.
server.rate-limit-suggest-defaults: Defaults: 60 requests per 20s, 120 per min per IP.
server.domain-block-desc: Hide search results from specific domains. One domain per line. Subdomains are included automatically. Wrap a line in /slashes/ for regex.
server.domain-block-regex-help: Regex entries must be wrapped in /slashes/ and use JavaScript RegExp syntax (no flags). They are matched against the URL hostname only (not the full URL). Example: /(^|\\.)instagram\\.com$/ blocks instagram.com and all subdomains; /^www\\.instagram\\.com$/ blocks only www.instagram.com.
server.domain-block-ui-desc: When enabled, authenticated users can block a domain directly from a kebab menu on each result row. Hidden on public or unauthenticated instances regardless of this setting.
server.domain-replace-desc: Replace domains in search result URLs. Use the format: source.com -> target.com (one per line). Subdomains are matched automatically, and the path is preserved.
server.domain-replace-ui-desc: When enabled, authenticated users can replace a domain directly from a kebab menu on each result row. Hidden on public or unauthenticated instances regardless of this setting.
server.domain-score-desc: Boost or demote domains in the result ranking. Each row is a domain and an integer; higher values rank higher, negative values rank lower. Subdomains are matched automatically.
server.domain-score-ui-desc: When enabled, authenticated users can score a domain directly from a kebab menu on each result row. Hidden on public or unauthenticated instances regardless of this setting.
server.oversized: <too large to render> {lines} entries ({size}). Edit this list directly in data/search/search-lists.json.
server.custom-css-desc: Inject custom CSS into every page. Loaded after theme and plugin styles, so it overrides them. Use !important when you need to beat selectors with higher specificity.
server.honeypot-desc: Protect your instance from bots and scrapers. Honeypot traps bait and block crawlers; the browser check verifies that requests come from a real browser by injecting a signed CSS token into every page.
server.honeypot-ban-duration-desc: How long a banned IP stays blocked. Set to 0 for a permanent ban.
server.honeypot-blocklist-desc: IPs currently blocked. Expired bans are removed automatically on the next request. Add an IP manually or unban entries below.
server.indexer-desc: Record results you actually see and re-surface them as a Degoog engine on later searches.
server.indexer-enable-desc: When on, search results are stored locally.
server.shortcuts-desc: Enable customisable keyboard shortcuts for this instance. When off, the Shortcuts section is hidden and no shortcuts are active.
server.shortcuts-enable-desc: Power-user shortcuts for focusing search, switching tabs, and navigating results. Configure them in the Shortcuts section.

proxy-test.not-enabled: Proxy is not enabled. Enable it and save before testing.
proxy-test.ip-unreachable: Could not reach IP check service. Check your network connection.
proxy-test.unreachable-hint: The proxy server could not be contacted. Check your proxy URL and that the proxy is running.
proxy-test.match-title: IPs match - proxy may not be working.
proxy-test.match-hint: Both routes returned the same IP. The proxy might not be changing your exit IP.
proxy-test.server-error: Server returned {status}. Make sure you are authenticated.

extensions.tabs-desc: Set the default enabled engines and customise the order of result tabs.
extensions.exposure-exposed: Browser may contact external services directly
extensions.exposure-safe: Browser requests go through the server
extensions.requires-newer-version: Requires a newer version of Degoog
extensions.no-engines: No search engines installed. Head to the {store} tab to browse and install some.
extensions.no-autocomplete: No autocomplete providers installed. Head to the {store} tab to browse and install some.

gate.desc: Enter the password to access settings.
gate.network-error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
gate.misconfigured: Settings authentication is misconfigured: the configured gate references a middleware that is not loaded. Contact the administrator to fix the gate setting or set DEGOOG_SETTINGS_PASSWORDS.
gate.generated-password-note: DEGOOG_SETTINGS_PASSWORDS is not set, so Degoog generated a temporary password for this run. Check the server logs for it. To intentionally disable this gate, set DEGOOG_DANGEROUSLY_NO_PASSWORD=true.

indexer.heading: Degoog Indexer
indexer.desc: Silently record every result you see and re-surface them on future searches.
indexer.enable-desc: When on, results from your searches are stored locally and re-surface as a Degoog engine.
indexer.public-export: Allow public download of this index
indexer.public-export-desc: When on, anyone can download this index from the public settings page (rate-limited). Off by default.
indexer.domain-allowlist-desc: One domain per line. When non-empty, only results from these domains are indexed. Leave blank to allow all.
indexer.domain-blocklist-desc: One domain per line. Results from these domains are never indexed.
indexer.word-blocklist-desc: One word per line. If a word appears in a result's title, snippet, or URL, that result is never indexed.
indexer.oversized: <too large to render> {lines} entries ({size}). Edit this list directly in data/indexer/indexer-config.json.
indexer.max-per-search-desc: Only the top N aggregated results after each search are indexed. 0 means no limit.
indexer.max-urls-desc: Cap canonical URL rows in the index. 0 means unlimited.
indexer.max-hits-desc: Cap links between queries and URLs. 0 means unlimited.
indexer.max-age-days-desc: Remove indexed results not seen in this many days. 0 means keep forever.
indexer.prune-enabled-desc: Delete oldest entries when URL or link caps are exceeded. Only applies when a cap is set.
indexer.fuzzy-enabled-desc: When on, similar past queries can surface URLs you have seen before.
indexer.query-limit-desc: How many indexed hits the Degoog engine returns for a single search.
indexer.export-modal-desc: Choose the engine type to export.
indexer.import-modal-desc: Import a SQLite index file into the PostgreSQL backend. Select the engine type and pick the .db file to upload.
indexer.import-missing: Please select a type and a file.
indexer.import-done: Done - {urls} URLs and {hits} links imported as type: {type}.
indexer.clear-modal-desc: This permanently deletes every row in your local index. Type CLEAR to confirm.
indexer.disabled: The indexer is disabled. Enable it under Server settings.

indexer-public.unavailable: The indexer is not enabled for public access on this instance.

store.desc: Add a git repository URL to browse and install plugins, themes, engines, and transports. Set repo-image in the repo’s package.json to show an image next to the URL.
store.empty-repos: No repositories added. Add a git repository URL to browse its plugins, themes, engines, and transports.
store.install-plugin-message: This plugin will run code on your server. Only install from sources you trust. Continue?
store.remove-repo-message: Remove this repository? You must uninstall any installed items first.

wizard.welcome-body: Let's get your instance set up. You can skip this tour at any time.
wizard.search-body: Type a query here and hit enter. Once you've set up engines, results come from the providers you choose.
wizard.goto-settings-body: Settings live behind this icon. Click Next and we'll take you straight to the store to install your first engine.
wizard.store-repos-body: Repositories are git URLs that expose engines, autocomplete providers, themes, plugins, and transports. Add one to populate the catalog below - then come back here when you find more.
wizard.store-engines-body: Degoog ships with no search engines installed - searches return nothing until you add at least one. We've filtered the catalog to engines below; pick the providers you want.
wizard.store-engines-hint: {count} engine(s) installed. At least one is required to continue.
wizard.store-autocomplete-body: Autocomplete providers power the live suggestions under the search bar - none ship by default. The catalog is filtered to autocomplete, install at least one for live suggestions.
wizard.store-themes-body: Themes restyle the whole UI. Install any you like from the filtered catalog, or skip and stick with the default.
wizard.store-plugins-body: Plugins add commands, slots, middleware and other features. Only install plugins from sources you trust - they run code on your server.
wizard.store-transports-body: Transports change how Degoog reaches the internet (direct, proxy, custom HTTP clients). Install one if you need outgoing requests routed differently.
wizard.engines-body: Toggle the engines you installed, group them, and set per-engine options. The engines you enable here are the ones that will run on every search.
wizard.autocomplete-body: Select which provider powers the search-bar suggestions. Enabling Rich suggestions will give you thumbnails and descriptions for the most relevant results.
wizard.server-body: Rate limits, language list, honeypot, proxy, API key. The defaults are sensible. Visit this tab when you need to harden the instance.
wizard.security-body: If this instance is reachable from the internet, lock down admin and store actions: set DEGOOG_SETTINGS_PASSWORDS, or set DEGOOG_PUBLIC_INSTANCE=true. With neither, anyone who can reach it can install extensions, which run code on your server.
wizard.done-body: That's the tour. Reopen it any time from the general tab if you forget where something lives.
wizard.restart-desc: Start over from the home page: search bar, settings, then the store, engines, and the rest of setup.

natural-language.label: Natural language
natural-language.description: When on, typing the trigger or phrase without ! runs the command and shows search results below.

score.label: Score
score.description: Result ranking multiplier for this engine. Higher values favour its results.

outgoing-transport.label: Outgoing HTTP client
outgoing-transport.description: The outgoing HTTP client to use for this engine.

slot-position.label: Position
slot-position.description: Where the slot content appears on the page.

shortcuts.heading: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.desc: Click a shortcut to record a new key combination. Press Escape while recording to cancel.

focus-search.label: Focus search
focus-search.desc: Move the cursor into the search box.

next-tab.label: Next tab
next-tab.desc: Switch to the next results tab.

prev-tab.label: Previous tab
prev-tab.desc: Switch to the previous results tab.

tab-by-number.label: Switch tab by number
tab-by-number.desc: Hold the recorded modifier and press 1-9 to jump to that tab.

go-home.label: Go to home
go-home.desc: Return to the Degoog home page.

result-down.label: Highlight next result
result-down.desc: Move focus to the next result link.

result-up.label: Highlight previous result
result-up.desc: Move focus to the previous result link.
